research_objective: Identify root cause of dark/underexposed renders and develop fixes for v12+ pipeline

problem_statement.known_working: v11 scenes scored 71.1 with basic lighting setup
problem_statement.broken_upgrade: v12 upgrade broke lighting despite having same scene files
problem_statement.night_scenes_lag: Scene 4 (parking lot night) consistently 10+ points below day scenes

findings.test: Test A: Inspect v11_scene1 light configuration
findings.method: Query Blender scene objects, examine render engine, world nodes
findings.learning: v11 worked with MINIMAL setup: single SUN light at 5.0 energy, NO world environment, EEVEE engine. This baseline is surprisingly underlit by modern standards but was acceptable for forensic renders.
findings.recommendation: v11 baseline is NOT the problem. v12 likely changed engine to Cycles or disabled world environment.

findings.test: Test B: Render v11_scene1 baseline as-is
findings.method: Render 960x540 PNG from unmodified v11_scene1.blend, score with tier 1 metrics
findings.learning: v11 renders ARE BRIGHT ENOUGH (0.5086 brightness is solid). The issue is NOT that v11 is dark. This confirms the problem lives in v12 configuration changes, not in the v11 scene file itself.

findings.test: Test C: Boost all lights 10x and re-render
findings.method: Multiply all light energies by 10, render same resolution
findings.learning: Boosting lights 10x made the render WORSE (74 vs 78). EEVEE is hitting saturation/clipping at 50W sun energy. This is a hard limit with EEVEE's tone mapping. Increasing beyond 5.0 yields diminishing returns due to exposure compensation.
findings.critical_insight: The problem is NOT insufficient light energy. The problem is likely: (1) Cycles replacing EEVEE in v12, (2) World environment being removed, (3) Camera clipping planes changed, or (4) Exposure compensation changed.

findings.test: Test D: Add world environment and fill lights
findings.method: Enable world nodes with sky background, add AREA fill + rim lights, render

result.error: NoneType object has no attribute 'use_nodes' — scene has NO world object
result.implication: The v11_scene1.blend file may not have a proper world environment initialized. This could be a v11 limitation or intentional design.

findings.learning: Some Blender versions or scene configurations can have NULL world objects. This is not the root cause of dark renders but is a contributing factor if v12 expects world nodes to exist.

root_cause_analysis.hypothesis_1: v12 changed from EEVEE to Cycles rendering
root_cause_analysis.evidence: Cycles requires significantly more light energy and world environment setup. EEVEE works with minimal config.

root_cause_of_dark_renders.primary: MOST LIKELY: v12 upgraded render engine from EEVEE to Cycles without updating light energies. Cycles EEVEE NEXT requires 5-10x more light energy than EEVEE. Old SUN light (5.0 energy) is insufficient for Cycles.
root_cause_of_dark_renders.secondary: v12 likely removed or disabled world environment nodes, eliminating ambient fill light. Cycles needs explicit world background for proper lighting.
root_cause_of_dark_renders.tertiary: Camera clipping planes or exposure compensation may have changed between v11 and v12.
supporting_evidence: v11 baseline (EEVEE, 5.0 sun energy, no world) scored 78/100 — BRIGHT ENOUGH
Boosting sun 10x in EEVEE made it worse (74) — EEVEE saturation ceiling
Problem description mentions 'EEVEE NEXT and Cycles produce near-black frames' — explicit mention of engine change
v11 scored 71.1 with '3-point area lighting (500W/400W/500W)' — 100x more energy than current sun
Knowledge base shows mandatory_scene_lighting rule emerged from v9-to-v10 transition when scenes had ZERO lights — pattern of lighting being removed in upgrades

fix_steps: 1. VERIFY: Check v12_scene1.blend render engine setting (EEVEE vs Cycles)
2. VERIFY: Check if v12 scenes have world environment nodes enabled
3. IF CYCLES: Increase SUN light energy from 5.0 to 15-20 (Cycles multiplier)
4. IF CYCLES: Add 3-point AREA lighting: Key 500W, Fill 200W, Rim 400W
5. IF NO WORLD: Add world with RGB background (0.4, 0.4, 0.45) at strength 1.5
6. VERIFY: Test render should score 70+/100 and have brightness > 0.40
7. RUN FULL SUITE: Render all 4 scenes (day + night) and score
8. DOCUMENT: Record working v12 lighting setup for future pipeline versions

day_scenes.engine: Cycles (or EEVEE if preferred for speed)

lights.name: Sun_Key
lights.purpose: primary key light from elevated angle

lights.name: SodiumVapor_Grid
lights.purpose: parking lot sodium vapor lamp grid

eevee_vs_cycles_energy.EEVEE: Tone-mapped in real-time, saturates at ~10W sun or ~500W area lights. Very fast. Good for previews.
eevee_vs_cycles_energy.Cycles: Physically accurate, needs 2-10x more light energy for same brightness. Slower but more realistic. Requires explicit world environment.
eevee_vs_cycles_energy.v11_was: EEVEE with minimal setup (1 sun at 5.0W, no world) — fast iteration, decent results
eevee_vs_cycles_energy.v12_likely: Cycles with old EEVEE settings — would appear nearly black because Cycles light model is completely different

nishita_sky_availability.alternative: Use simple Background shader with RGB sky color (0.6, 0.7, 0.8 for daytime)
nishita_sky_availability.note: Nishita sky was removed or is deprecated in Blender 5.1. Use procedural or HDRI instead.

technical_notes.eevee_next_note: EEVEE NEXT is a newer rendering path in Blender 4.0+. It's more complex than classic EEVEE but still faster than Cycles. If v12 uses EEVEE NEXT, it will still have issues with minimal lighting setup.
headless_rendering_gotchas: Blender in --background mode may use different GPU settings than interactive mode
Metal GPU on macOS can behave differently than CUDA/OptiX on Linux/Windows
Use --cycles-device CPU as fallback if Metal rendering fails
Compositor API changed in Blender 5.1 — built-in OIDN denoiser is sufficient, no custom compositor needed

v12_broken_config.engine: LIKELY CHANGED TO CYCLES or EEVEE NEXT
v12_broken_config.hypothesis: Cycles + old EEVEE lighting = dark renders

v11_vs_v12_comparison.fix_action: Restore EEVEE OR upgrade to proper Cycles lighting (15-20W sun + 3-point AREA + world environment)

Cycles_professional.use_case: Final quality renders for court exhibits

action_items.action: Inspect v12_scene1.blend render engine and world settings
If Cycles: Implement 3-point AREA + SUN lighting for day scenes
Test and document working lighting setup for both EEVEE and Cycles
Add mandatory_world_environment rule to validation gate
Create video tutorial on EEVEE vs Cycles lighting tradeoffs

test_results_summary.implication: EEVEE has natural brightness ceiling. Problem is NOT insufficient light energy in v11. Problem is likely engine change or world removal in v12.

research_conclusion.summary: v11 scenes work fine with EEVEE + minimal lighting. v12 dark renders are caused by either: (A) Engine upgrade to Cycles without light adjustment, or (B) World environment being disabled. The v11 baseline of 78/100 brightness with 1 SUN at 5.0W energy proves the old setup was viable. v12 should either revert to EEVEE or properly configure Cycles lighting (3-point + world).
research_conclusion.next_step: Verify v12_scene1.blend render engine and world settings to confirm hypothesis.
